meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't deploy apps. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: API Integration Guide: Deploy Your First AI App (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment. Use this advanced API integration guide to master Antigravity and Claude Code for a seamless, production-ready AI application.
content.answer_block: Deployment failures usually stem from misconfigured environment variables or broken API handshakes. By decoupling your business logic from the infrastructure using Antigravity and automating the deployment pipeline with Claude Code, you transform a brittle local environment into a resilient, production-grade API architecture that scales effortlessly.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks architecting the perfect AI application, yet the moment you push to production, the screen goes dark. The error logs are cryptic, the API keys are leaking, and your local success feels like a distant memory. The 'can't deploy' wall is the rite of passage that separates hobbyists from professional engineers.

This isn't about trial and error; it’s about engineering discipline. By leveraging high-level orchestration tools like Antigravity and the autonomous coding capabilities of Claude Code, you can bridge the gap between your local terminal and the live web. It’s time to stop fighting your infrastructure and start shipping code that stays online.

steps.title: Environment Decoupling
steps.description: Isolate your API credentials from your codebase to prevent leaks and configuration drift.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my current configuration module to use a secure, environment-variable-first approach with strict validation schemas.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement dotenv and schema validation for all API keys'

steps.title: API Handshake Optimization
steps.description: Implement robust error handling for your third-party API calls to prevent deployment-time timeouts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware wrapper for my API calls that includes exponential backoff, circuit breaking, and detailed status logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'add retry logic and circuit breaking to all network requests'

steps.title: Containerizing the Logic
steps.description: Package your application into a lightweight, portable container that mirrors your production environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-optimized Dockerfile for my Node.js/Python stack, ensuring minimal image size and security best practices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate Dockerfile and .dockerignore for current project'

steps.title: Automated Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Use Claude Code to define the CI/CD configuration that triggers a deployment upon successful commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, tests, and deploys my container to the cloud provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'setup CI/CD pipeline for automated cloud deployment'

steps.title: Post-Deployment Smoke Testing
steps.description: Automate health checks to ensure your API endpoints are live and responding correctly after deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of smoke tests that ping my critical endpoints and verify response status codes post-deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'create post-deployment health check script'

pro_tips: Always use a .env.example file to document required keys without exposing real credentials.
Implement request ID tracing to debug API failures across distributed services easily.
Set up your API rate limits on the server side to protect your budget from recursive loops.
Use mock servers in your local environment to test API edge cases without incurring costs.
Always monitor your API latency; a slow response is often perceived by users as a broken app.
content.next_steps: Once your app is live, focus on setting up observability tools like Sentry or Datadog to catch bugs before your users do.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the technical blueprint to deploy with confidence and master the API integration lifecycle. Stop letting deployment errors dictate your progress. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

acceptedAnswer.text: Isolate your API credentials from your codebase to prevent leaks and configuration drift.
Implement robust error handling for your third-party API calls to prevent deployment-time timeouts.
Package your application into a lightweight, portable container that mirrors your production environment.
Use Claude Code to define the CI/CD configuration that triggers a deployment upon successful commit.
Automate health checks to ensure your API endpoints are live and responding correctly after deployment.
